name: Taegis XDR
description: Sophos Taegis is a cloud-native extended detection and response (XDR) platform that unifies endpoint, server, firewall, and cloud data for faster detection and response. Standard data retention (included at no extra cost for raw logs/telemetry from endpoint, network, cloud, etc.) is 12 months (365 days); options to extend retention beyond that are available.

detection-response.note: Unified XDR with EDR, NDR, and identity correlation

siem.note: Full SIEM with long-term retention and search

managed-soc-mdr.note: Managed XDR and MDR services available

integrations.note: Broad ecosystem; native Sophos stack integration

log-storage.note: Standard data retention is 12 months (365 days) at no extra cost; options to extend available.

strengths: Single platform for EDR, NDR, SIEM, and MDR
weaknesses: Less brand recognition than some legacy vendors
differentiators: Unified data lake and native XDR story
MDR and managed services from same vendor
notes: Taegis is positioned as a modern, cloud-first alternative to legacy SIEM and point EDR.

name: Microsoft Sentinel
description: Microsoft Sentinel is a cloud-native SIEM and SOAR solution that ingests data from Microsoft 365, Azure, and many third-party sources.

detection-response.note: Strong with Defender stack; SOAR and automation

siem.note: Full SIEM; scales with log volume

managed-soc-mdr.note: MDR via partners and Microsoft services

integrations.note: Very large connector ecosystem; Azure/M365 native

pricing-model.note: Usage-based; can be complex to estimate

log-storage.note: Standard data retention is 90 days; longer retention available as a paid option.

strengths: Deep integration with Microsoft 365 and Azure
Familiar for organizations already on Microsoft
weaknesses: Pricing can be unpredictable at scale
Best value when heavily invested in Microsoft stack
differentiators: Single pane for Microsoft security portfolio
notes: Sentinel is a leading choice for enterprises standardizing on Microsoft security.

name: CrowdStrike Falcon
description: CrowdStrike Falcon is an endpoint-centric XDR platform known for lightweight agents, threat intelligence, and cloud-native architecture.

detection-response.note: Industry-leading EDR and XDR; fast detection

siem.note: Falcon LogScale adds SIEM; less traditional SIEM focus

managed-soc-mdr.note: Falcon Complete and OverWatch MDR offerings

pricing-model.note: Subscription tiers; premium for full stack

log-storage.note: Standard data retention is 90 days; longer retention requires add-on.

strengths: Lightweight agent and strong endpoint visibility
weaknesses: Cost can be high for full platform
differentiators: Single lightweight agent for multiple modules
notes: CrowdStrike is often chosen for best-in-class EDR and MDR; SIEM is an add-on.

name: Palo Alto Cortex XSIAM
description: Palo Alto Cortex XSIAM is an advanced security platform that integrates detection and response capabilities with security information and event management (SIEM) functionalities. It leverages AI and machine learning to enhance threat detection and streamline incident response.

detection-response.note: Highly effective in threat detection and response.

siem.note: Robust SIEM capabilities with advanced analytics.

managed-soc-mdr.note: Offers managed services for enhanced security.

integrations.note: Seamless integration with various security tools.

log-storage.note: Standard data retention is 90 days; options to extend may be available at extra cost.

strengths: Strong integration with existing security tools
AI-driven analytics for faster incident response
weaknesses: Higher cost compared to some competitors
differentiators: Unified platform for XDR and SIEM

name: Rapid7
description: Rapid7 provides a comprehensive security platform offering solutions for vulnerability management, detection and response, and SIEM. It is widely used for its user-friendly interface and strong integration capabilities.

detection-response.note: Strong detection and response capabilities with XDR and MDR offerings.

managed-soc-mdr.note: Offers managed detection and response services.

integrations.note: Extensive integrations with third-party tools and cloud services.

deployment-model.note: Primarily cloud-based, with some hybrid options.

pricing-model.note: Subscription pricing is transparent and scalable.

target-customer-size.note: Best suited for midmarket and some enterprise customers.

log-storage.note: Standard data retention is 90 days; longer retention may require additional licensing.

weaknesses: May be costly for smaller organizations
differentiators: Unified platform for vulnerability management and SIEM
Strong managed detection and response services

name: Sumo Logic
description: Sumo Logic is a cloud-native machine data analytics platform providing real-time insights for security, operations, and business intelligence. It is widely used for log management, SIEM, and security analytics.

detection-response.note: Adequate SIEM and analytics, but EDR/XDR and incident response are not standout features.

target-customer-size.note: Best fit for midmarket and enterprise

strengths: Strong log management and search capabilities
weaknesses: Can become expensive at high data volumes
Advanced features may require steep learning curve
differentiators: Fast, scalable log ingestion and search
